# US Stock Watcher Next-Session Forecast — Monday 7/20

**[Monday 7/20 session outlook] Bullish lean — Moderate / selective risk-on · composite +21 · confidence 40%**

**🔮 Next-session forecast** — The next U.S. regular session is Monday, July 20, opening at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST). This brief is issued while markets are closed and is oriented entirely toward that upcoming session.

*Data as of: 2026-07-19T06:10:13 · Data quality: fresh*

## Executive Summary

Heading into Monday's open, the composite market-state score stands at +21 out of 100, placing the setup in "Moderate / selective risk-on" territory with 40% confidence, based on four measured components. Several inputs remain unmeasured for this read — Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning have all been reweighted out of the current calculation — so the lean should be treated as directional rather than definitive as trading resumes Monday.

## Market Diagnosis

**Indices.** Friday's closing levels set the base for Monday's open: the S&P 500 finished at 7,457.69, down 1.01% on the day; the Nasdaq-100 closed at 28,592.66, off 1.49%; the Dow ended at 52,146.42, down 0.77%; and the Russell 2000 settled at 2,962.22, down 0.42%.

**Market drivers.** Four forces are shaping the setup for Monday's session. Volatility and index trend both register as headwinds, while the yield curve stands out as a supportive factor. The US dollar rounds out the picture as an additional headwind.

**Breadth & concentration.** The overall market-state score of +21/100 continues to point to a "Moderate / selective risk-on" posture, with confidence held at 40% across the four components currently measured. As noted above, Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning remain unmeasured and reweighted out, which caps conviction in this read heading into the new week.

**Sector & rotation.** Leadership is concentrated in Health Care, Energy, and Financials, while Consumer Discretionary, Materials, and Technology are lagging — a rotation pattern worth watching for continuation or reversal signals when trading opens Monday.

**Rates, dollar & commodities.** The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.57%. The Dollar Index (DXY) is at 100.75, up 0.02% on the day. In commodities, WTI crude has jumped 3.58% to 81.78, and gold has advanced 0.83% to 4,018.80.

## Outlook

**Short term.** With trading set to resume Monday, July 20 (9:30 AM ET / 22:30 KST), the short-term outlook carries a constructive bias consistent with the "Moderate / selective risk-on" market state and its 40% confidence reading. That said, this lean would flip should the overall composite score cross back below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term view mirrors the near-term stance: a constructive bias tied to the same "Moderate / selective risk-on" read at 40% confidence, with the same trigger for reversal — a move of the overall score across the zero line.

**Medium-to-long term.** Extending further out, the medium-to-long-term outlook likewise leans constructive on the back of the "Moderate / selective risk-on" state and 40% confidence, again subject to invalidation if the composite score turns negative.

## Top News

Coverage feeding into this brief includes: Munshoot AI's Kimi K3 shaking global semiconductor stocks (조선일보); Yardeni QuickTakes' "US Market Call: Hanging Out At 7,500 And 4.50%"; SK Hynix reportedly considering a U.S. factory for price relief (조선일보); The Straits Times on AI demand driving Singapore GDP even as Q2 growth eases and global chipmakers take a hit; 매일경제 reporting Chey Tae-won's view that AI semiconductor demand will double next year, describing the global market as being in a state of chaos; and Michael Hudson's piece, "The K-Shaped Collapse of the US Economy."
